sn.s: Sit em Striit fühl ich öis nächer binand.
sn.e: Since the argument I feel us closer together.

sn.s: Mir redet jetz über Sache, wo mer vorhär verschwige händ.
sn.e: We now talk about things we kept quiet before.

sn.s: Ich verzell ihre vo minere Angscht, nie würkli daheim z'sii.
sn.e: I tell her about my fear of never truly being at home.

sn.s: "Du bisch daheim, wenn du bi mir bisch," seit sie.
sn.e: "You are home when you're with me," she says.

sn.s: Söttigi Wort höred mer nöd alli Tag.
sn.e: You don't hear words like that every day.

sn.s: Sie teilt au ihri Sorge mit mir, ganz ehrlich.
sn.e: She also shares her worries with me, very honestly.

sn.s: Vertroue baut mer nöd a einem Tag uf.
sn.e: You don't build trust in one day.

sn.s: Es wachst langsam, wie en Boum.
sn.e: It grows slowly, like a tree.

sn.s: Mir mached Plän für d'Zuekunft, chlini und grossi.
sn.e: We make plans for the future, small ones and big ones.

sn.s: "Villicht göhmer mal zäme uf Reise," sägt sie.
sn.e: "Maybe we'll travel together sometime," she says.

sn.s: Ich gseh in ihre öpper, wo zu mir ghört.
sn.e: I see in her someone who belongs with me.

sn.s: Mit ihre cha ich ich säuber sii, ganz ohni Angscht.
sn.e: With her I can be myself, completely without fear.

sn.s: Das isch mir s'Wichtigschte vo allem.
sn.e: That is the most important thing of all to me.

sn.s: So fühlt sich das aa, wenn me sicher isch binand.
sn.e: This is what it feels like when you're secure together.
